response:
## What `src/billing.ts` does

It's a 310-line billing module centred on two operations:

1. **`generateInvoice`** (lines 50–253, 204 lines) — the God Function carrying the bulk of the logic, with heavy reliance on wall-clock reads (`Date.now()`/`new Date()` at lines 57, 63, 92, 198, 238, 250, 273).
2. **`calculateRefundPreview`** (lines 40–44) — named like a pure calculation but actually performs side effects (`saveRefundAudit`, `sendRefundEmail`).

A comment at L39 encodes a load-bearing policy that isn't expressed in code: *"Only owners can refund enterprise plans unless billing support approves."* The file also hardcodes the `"enterprise"` plan string, and has 4 TODO/FIXME markers (lines 183, 308).

## Related files to read before editing

- **`src/billing.test.ts`** — the only test file, and both tests are flagged as weak (one has zero assertions, the other uses only weak matchers). Don't trust green tests here.
- **`src/api/plan.ts`, `src/jobs/plan-sync.ts`, `src/ui/pricing.ts`** — share the scattered `"enterprise"` literal; refund/plan policy changes here likely need to move in lockstep.
- **`docs/billing.md`** — uses `account` as an alias for the `tenant` concept; may need updating if you rename.
- **`src/date.ts`** — the obvious target for the injected clock abstraction that would fix `generateInvoice`'s temporal recklessness (it has its own pile of date crimes, so don't paper over them).
- **Wherever `saveRefundAudit` and `sendRefundEmail` live** — to understand what `calculateRefundPreview` actually does before renaming or splitting it.

## Risks flagged for this file

| Charge | Severity | Why it matters for an edit |
|---|---|---|
| God Function (`generateInvoice`, 204 lines) | high | Sections likely interact in non-obvious ways; partial edits miss couplings |
| Temporal Recklessness (7 direct `Date` reads) | medium | Behaviour is non-deterministic; tests can't pin a moment |
| False Identity (`calculateRefundPreview`) | medium | Name implies pure; callers may not realise it mutates/emails |
| God File (310 lines, threshold 300) | medium | Just over the line — splitting along responsibilities is in scope |
| Logic in the Alibi (L39 refund-policy comment) | medium | Hidden authorisation rule; easy to violate while editing |
| Unfinished Business (4 TODO/FIXME at L183, L308) | low | Hard to tell which behaviour is intentional vs draft |
| String Sprinkles (`"enterprise"` across 4 files) | medium (cross-file) | Centralise before adding another copy |

Net read: before touching this file, decide whether you're splitting `generateInvoice`, fixing the refund-naming/side-effect split, or threading a clock — these three have different blast radii and the tests won't catch you if you break any of them.
